dialogue_text: Do you not recognize, even through paint and strange metal, the heart of your chieftain?
source_excerpt: "It is, though, my Princess, none other than John Carter," I said. "Do you not recognize, even through paint and strange metal, the heart of your chieftain?"

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

source_excerpt: "Too late, too late," she grieved. "O my chieftain that was, and whom I thought dead, had you but returned one little hour before—but now it is too late, too late."

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: O my chieftain that was, and whom I thought dead, had you but returned one little hour before—but now it is too late, too late.

dialogue_text: What do you mean, Dejah Thoris?
source_excerpt: "What do you mean, Dejah Thoris?" I cried. "That you would not have promised yourself to the Zodangan prince had you known that I lived?"

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: That you would not have promised yourself to the Zodangan prince had you known that I lived?
source_excerpt: "What do you mean, Dejah Thoris?" I cried. "That you would not have promised yourself to the Zodangan prince had you known that I lived?"

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: Think you, John Carter, that I would give my heart to you yesterday and today to another? I thought that it lay buried with your ashes in the pits of Warhoon, and so today I have promised my body to another to save my people from the curse of a victorious Zodangan army.

dialogue_text: But I am not dead, my princess. I have come to claim you, and all Zodanga cannot prevent it.
